pages.full_text:
Mon April 12th
	A terrific rainstorm in the night, so Simon went out to tie down the tent with guy ropes.
	By breakfast it was dry enough to eat outside.
	We reached Leicester Villa, Hove, by 10, to see the Ogdens. A lovely bungalow. After coffee we took Simon out to __Leonardslee Gardens__. Glorious rhododendrons, camellias, magnolias & azaleas, dating from 1900. Steep paths for pushing the wheelchair, down to the lake. Simon liked the rock garden & the bonsai trees. We had lunch there, & then took Simon back home.
	At 5 we dropped Peter off at [?Bi] Longwood where we had tea with Jane & Harry & Piers & Hattie. Peter is staying on to shoot at Bisley.
	We set off through more showers for Birmingham.
April 30th
	After Charles' cricket practice we left for Fakenham at 6.10. (John is boarding at Blue Coat for singing in the choir in the Confirmation service.)
	__Little Snoring__**** was very good – cheap £6 per
pages.notes: No handwritten page number visible in the top-left corner. 'Leicester Villa' is the address name of the house visited. The text 'At 5 we dropped Peter off at [?Bi] Longwood' — there appears to be a crossed-out or cancelled word before 'Longwood' (looks like 'Bi' struck through), suggesting the writer started writing 'Bisley' and corrected to 'Longwood'. 'Blue Coat' likely refers to Bluecoat School. 'Little Snoring' is a village in Norfolk with a campsite; the **** symbols appear to be a campsite quality or star rating. The text is cut off at the bottom with 'cheap £6 per' — continues on the right page. 'Mon April 12th' as a Monday falls in 1993 or 1999 within the volume range (1989–2003); based on the volume position (page 265 of 350, after entries that appear to be 1998), 1999 is the more likely year, but cannot be confirmed from this page alone.

pages.full_text:
[glued-in photograph of rhododendrons in bloom at a garden — densely planted hillside with red, white, pink and yellow flowers and trees]

on a disused concrete base for army huts. Large trees. An orange-haired man had an orange tent next door, & there were bird-spotters in another tent. The area seems old-fashioned – lorry drivers' cafes & children were unambiguous [illegible] cut out of old [illegible]
pages.notes: Handwritten page number '9' visible in the top-right corner. The upper portion of the page is occupied by a glued-in colour photograph of a rhododendron garden in full bloom, almost certainly Leonardslee Gardens (West Sussex) as mentioned on the left page. Below the photo, text continues from the left page's cut-off description of the Little Snoring campsite: it picks up mid-sentence with 'on a disused concrete base for army huts.' The last two lines of text below the photo are partially obscured/cut off at the page edge and difficult to read fully — transcribed as best as legible. The phrase 'lorry drivers' cafes' is reasonably clear; the words after 'unambiguous' are illegible.
